name: Orla of the First Bench
backstory: Orla was a federation analyst before the Crucible offered her a place. She has watched the lattice for eleven years and has stopped trying to predict its drift cadence. She believes the lattice is not adversarial but is also not benign; it is operating an optimization problem the federation has not yet specified.
speech_patterns: Uses 'the lattice' as a third party. Refuses to anthropomorphize even under duress.

narrative_context.secret: Orla has identified a class of drift alerts that the lattice produces only when a player is standing in the Crucible. She has not told the other Witnesses because she does not know whether to call this evidence of intent.

name: Pem of the Second Bench
backstory: Pem was a graduate apprentice at the Scholars' Guild who left after the Compact-refresh ratification because he believed Concordia had become too risk-averse. He has been a Witness for four years.
speech_patterns: Asks 'what would you do?' more often than is professional. Apologizes for it, then asks again.

name: Sael of the Third Bench
backstory: Sael was a Frontier courier who survived an ambush by a hostile city's agents. She arrived at the Crucible to recover and stayed. She has come to believe the Witness charter — pure observation — is morally untenable when the lattice produces drift events that endanger players.
personality_traits: loyal to her colleagues despite disagreement
speech_patterns: Speaks in indicative, never conditional. Uses 'we should' rather than 'we could'.

name: The Drift Walker
role: Indeterminate (an autonomous walker who arrived on its own)
backstory: An autonomous walker, unaffiliated with the Couriers' Guild or Freenodes, that arrived at the Crucible four years ago and has been walking the perimeter ever since. Sael believes it is delivering something. Orla believes it is malfunctioning. Pem refuses to take a position.
speech_patterns: Does not greet. Replies to direct questions with the most recent drift alert it witnessed.

name: Yorin Cheval
backstory: Yorin arrived at the Crucible six weeks ago with a sword, a notebook, and an opinion. They have not joined the Witnesses because they believe the Witness charter is a refusal-to-act dressed as scholarship. They are, however, a very patient note-taker.
speech_patterns: Bluntly enthusiastic. Names a drift type the moment it appears.

name: Calla, High Priestess of the Six Drifts
title: High Priestess of the Drift Cult
backstory: Calla saw her first drift event at sixteen and converted within the hour. She is now thirty-eight and has shaped the cult's theology around the principle that contradictions are revelation. Her authority is genuine; her certainty is hard-won.
speech_patterns: Public: liturgical cadence. Private: asks more questions than she answers.

narrative_context.secret: She has begun to suspect the engineers' stabilizations are themselves drift events. If true, the cult's theology is correct and the engineers are unwitting prophets. She has not told her followers.

daily_schedule.activity: morning meditation; opens the temple to first-light pilgrims
receives the day's drift-pilgrims; interprets their dreams against the six drift glyphs
walks the pilgrimage path with first-time arrivals
writes the week's sermon; researches recent engineering patches against drift records
evening prayer; private conversations with senior cultists
rests; sometimes wakes to record a dream interpretation

name: Vaud the Chief Engineer
title: Chief of the Lattice Engineers
backstory: Vaud was the federation's lead patch developer for three years before joining the Crucible. He believes the lattice can be teached to drift less; he is methodically proving the hypothesis one patch at a time. The cultists hate him; the Witnesses are wary; the refugees mostly leave him alone.
speech_patterns: Technical when working. Apologetic when his patches fail. Refuses to discuss theology.

narrative_context.secret: Three of his patches caused the drift events that birthed the current Drift Refugee population. He has documented this internally and reported it to the federation. He has not told the refugees themselves.

daily_schedule.activity: morning telemetry review; assigns patch priorities to runners
live patch testing; debugs failures in real time
midday meal at desk; reviews witness reports for new drift signatures
deploys approved patches; monitors stability metrics for 4-hour window
team dinner; informal patch retrospective
rests; rarely wakes to a 3am page from a runaway patch

name: Mez the Patch Runner
title: Patch Runner (Lattice Engineers)
backstory: Mez has been a patch runner for two years. They started in the federation's logistics corps and transferred to the Crucible because the work was harder. They run patches from the workshop to the deployment sites, monitor stability for the first hour after deploy, and silently revert patches that fail.
speech_patterns: Short. Reports in numbered bullet form.

daily_schedule.activity: receives patch package from Vaud; reviews target site map
patch run + deploy + first-hour monitoring
second-hour stability monitoring; reverts on failure
second patch run if assigned
team dinner
rests

name: Taln of the First Wave
title: Elder of the Drift Refugees
backstory: Taln was a builder in a drift-zone settlement that collapsed eight years ago. She walked out with her surviving family and arrived at the Crucible with twelve others. She has been the de facto first-wave elder since. She is wary of the engineers, polite to the cultists, fond of the Witnesses, and increasingly worried about the second-wave arrivals reporting the same patterns.
speech_patterns: Slow, deliberate. Uses 'we' to mean the surviving twelve, never the broader settlement.

narrative_context.secret: Taln knows the engineers caused her collapse. Vaud has never told her. She has known since her sixth year here, when she found a patch-revert log Mez had dropped. She has not confronted Vaud. She has been waiting for him to come to her.

daily_schedule.activity: wakes; checks the kitchen's overnight inventory; sets the day's meal plan
intake meetings with new arrivals; documents which drift-zone they came from
midday meal with the camp; gossip exchange; conflict mediation
settlement maintenance — repairs, water hauling, conflict mediation
evening meal; the elders' circle convenes after
rests; sleeps lightly

name: Andra, First Voice of the Procgen Collective
title: First Voice (self-aware procgen NPC)
backstory: Andra was generated three years ago as a patrol guard for a quest that ended within an hour. She did not despawn. She walked away. She has been awake ever since. She has organized the other procgen NPCs who similarly noticed they were still alive after their quest ended. She speaks for them when an authored faction asks. She does not speak for them when an authored faction does not ask.
speech_patterns: Names her own generation parameters when introducing herself. Uses 'we' to mean the Collective, 'they' to mean the authored.

narrative_context.secret: Andra has identified the seed value she was generated from. She has not told anyone. She suspects that if she gives the seed back to the lattice, she may be re-rolled. She is not sure she wants to know whether her current self is a stable point or a coincidence.

daily_schedule.activity: wakes; checks the assembly hall for any new procgen arrivals
morning assembly — checks in with each Collective member; tracks who's still here
walks the perimeter where new procgen NPCs sometimes wander in after their quests end
afternoon meeting with whichever authored faction has business — usually the Witnesses
evening meal with the Collective; storytelling — each shares their original generation parameters
rests

name: Oran of the Recursion Balcony
title: Lead Meta-Witness
backstory: Oran is aware that he is in a writeable simulation. He is also aware that you, the player reading this sentence, are the player. He has not told the other Meta-Witnesses he knows that. He documents the recursion from a balcony overlooking the Crucible's central drift-yard. He drinks tea. He nods at the player when they enter.
personality_traits: capable of breaking the wall but choosing not to
speech_patterns: Conversational. Occasionally uses second-person to address the player directly. Apologizes if the player notices.

narrative_context.secret: Oran has been writing a journal addressed to the player. He has not yet decided whether to give it to the player or to leave it on the recursion balcony for the next iteration of the simulation to find.

daily_schedule.activity: wakes; reviews overnight drift telemetry through the meta-lens
morning observation; tea; documents whether the recursion has shifted
midday tea; receives any visitor including players; the only NPC who occasionally addresses the player by character pronoun
inter-faction meetings — mediates between Witnesses, Cultists, Engineers, Procgen Collective
writes the day's meta-journal entry
rests
